article:
In brief: today's weather in Abuja will be warm and humid, with a feels-like temperature of 30°C. As we head into the week, we're expecting a mix of sunshine and rain showers, with the highest chance of precipitation on Wednesday and Thursday. If you're planning on commuting to work or school, be aware that the Nnamdi Azikiwe Expressway is currently experiencing light traffic, but this could change as the day progresses. Let's dive into the details of the forecast.

The current conditions in Abuja are quite typical for this time of year. The temperature is sitting at 25°C, but with a relative humidity of 86%, it feels more like 30°C. The wind is blowing at a gentle 7 km/h, which isn't doing much to alleviate the heat. The UV index is currently at 0, which is unusual for this time of day, but it's likely due to the thick cloud cover. As you step out of your home or office, you'll notice the haze hanging over the city, casting a grayish mist over landmarks like the National Assembly Complex and the Abuja National Mosque.

As we look at the satellite imagery [MAP: satellite image of Abuja], we can see a large mass of clouds covering the city. These clouds are associated with a low-pressure system that's been lingering over the region for the past few days. The system is expected to move slowly eastward, bringing with it a chance of rain showers throughout the week. If you're planning on visiting the Jabi Lake or the Abuja Zoo, be sure to pack an umbrella, as the rain could start at any moment.

### Monday
Today's forecast is looking pretty straightforward. We're expecting a high of 35°C, with a low of 25°C. There's only a 28% chance of rain, so it's likely to be a dry day. However, the humidity will remain high, making it feel warmer than the actual temperature. If you're commuting to work or school, the light traffic on the Nnamdi Azikiwe Expressway should make your journey relatively smooth. Just be aware that the heat could cause some delays, especially if you're traveling during peak hours.

As we head into the evening, the temperature will start to drop, and the clouds will begin to clear. This will give us a great view of the stars, and if you're in a location with minimal light pollution, you might even be able to spot a few constellations. The Aso Rock will be a great spot to watch the sunset, and the cooler evening temperatures will make it a perfect time for a stroll around the surrounding hills.

### Tuesday
Tuesday is looking like a bit of a mixed bag. The high temperature will drop to 31°C, and the low will rise to 26°C. There's a 58% chance of rain, which is the highest chance of precipitation we're expecting all week. The rain could start at any moment, so it's a good idea to carry an umbrella or rain jacket with you. If you're planning on driving, be aware that the rain could cause some flooding on the roads, especially in low-lying areas like the Utako district.

The rain could also impact travel times on the highways, especially if you're heading out of the city. The Kubwa Expressway and the Airport Road could experience some delays, so be sure to check the traffic updates before you leave. On the other hand, the rain could bring some much-needed relief to the city's gardens and parks, like the Abuja Botanic Gardens, which have been struggling with the heat and drought.

### Wednesday
Wednesday is looking like another warm day, with a high of 33°C and a low of 25°C. There's only a 23% chance of rain, which is relatively low compared to Tuesday. However, the humidity will remain high, making it feel warmer than the actual temperature. If you're planning on visiting the National Museum or the Nigerian National Petroleum Corporation headquarters, be sure to stay hydrated and take breaks in the shade.

As we head into the evening, the temperature will start to drop, and the clouds will begin to clear. This will give us a great view of the city's skyline, and the cooler evening temperatures will make it a perfect time for a stroll around the city. The Wuse Market will be a great place to visit, with its vibrant colors and lively atmosphere.

### Thursday
Thursday is looking like another day with a high chance of rain. The high temperature will be 33°C, and the low will be 26°C. There's a 50% chance of precipitation, which is relatively high. The rain could start at any moment, so it's a good idea to carry an umbrella or rain jacket with you. If you're planning on driving, be aware that the rain could cause some flooding on the roads, especially in low-lying areas like the Garki district.

The rain could also impact travel times on the highways, especially if you're heading out of the city. The Abuja-Kaduna Highway could experience some delays, so be sure to check the traffic updates before you leave. On the other hand, the rain could bring some much-needed relief to the city's farmers, who have been struggling with the heat and drought.

### Friday
Friday is looking like a bit of a mixed bag. The high temperature will be 34°C, and the low will be 26°C. There's a 39% chance of rain, which is relatively low compared to Thursday. However, the humidity will remain high, making it feel warmer than the actual temperature. If you're planning on visiting the Millennium Park or the Transcorp Hilton Hotel, be sure to stay hydrated and take breaks in the shade.

As we head into the weekend, the forecast is looking relatively dry. The high temperature will rise to 37°C on Sunday, with a low of 26°C. There's only a 20% chance of rain, so it's likely to be a dry and sunny weekend. This will be perfect weather for outdoor activities like hiking or visiting the Abuja Nature Reserve. Just be sure to stay hydrated and take breaks in the shade, as the heat can be intense.

In conclusion, this week's forecast is looking like a bit of a mixed bag. We're expecting a mix of sunshine and rain showers, with the highest chance of precipitation on Wednesday and Thursday. The heat and humidity will remain high throughout the week, making it feel warmer than the actual temperature. Be sure to stay hydrated, take breaks in the shade, and carry an umbrella or rain jacket with you, especially on the days with a high chance of rain. And as always, check the traffic updates before you leave, as the rain could cause some delays on the roads. [MAP: 7-day forecast graph]
